sentences.base_language: The job interview
sentences.target_language: La entrevista de trabajo
sentences.grammarNote:
**La entrevista** = the interview — from **entre** (between) + **vista** (view/sight): a "between-views" face-to-face meeting.
**De trabajo** = job/work (specifying what kind of interview) — same pattern: **ropa de trabajo** (work clothes), **reunión de trabajo** (work meeting).

sentences.base_language: I had been preparing for this interview for two weeks
sentences.target_language: Llevaba dos semanas preparándome para esta entrevista
sentences.grammarNote:
**Llevaba + gerund** = I had been doing (duration in the past) — the past equivalent of **lleva + gerund**.
**Llevaba dos semanas preparándome** = I had been preparing for two weeks (and still was at that point).
**Preparándome** — reflexive gerund: **prepararse** (to prepare oneself) → **preparándome** (me is attached to the gerund).
**Esta entrevista** — demonstrative **esta** (this, feminine) agrees with **entrevista**.

sentences.base_language: The company was one of the most prestigious in the country
sentences.target_language: La empresa era una de las más prestigiosas del país
sentences.grammarNote:
**Era** = was — imperfect tense of **ser**, used for descriptions and background in the past.
**Una de las más prestigiosas** = one of the most prestigious — superlative construction: **una de las más + adjective**.
**Prestigiosas** agrees with **empresas** (implied feminine plural).
**Del país** = in the country — **del** = de + el (mandatory contraction).

sentences.base_language: I arrived fifteen minutes early so they wouldn't think I was irresponsible
sentences.target_language: Llegué quince minutos antes para que no pensaran que era irresponsable
sentences.grammarNote:
**Llegué** = I arrived — preterite of **llegar** (regular -ar, but **g → gu** before **e**: **llegué**).
**Para que + subjunctive** = so that — **para que** always triggers the subjunctive: **para que no pensaran** (so they wouldn't think).
**Pensaran** = imperfect subjunctive of **pensar** — used because the main verb is in the past.
**Era irresponsable** = I was irresponsible — imperfect for a state/description in the past.

sentences.base_language: The receptionist told me to have a seat and that someone would be right with me
sentences.target_language: La recepcionista me dijo que tomara asiento y que alguien me atendería enseguida
sentences.grammarNote:
**Me dijo que + imperfect subjunctive** — reported speech in the past: when the main verb is past, the reported command/request uses the imperfect subjunctive.
**Tomara** = imperfect subjunctive of **tomar** — the reported instruction: "take a seat".
**Atendería** = would attend (to me) — conditional tense, used in reported speech for future-in-the-past.
**Enseguida** = right away/immediately — a common adverb.

sentences.base_language: While I was waiting I went over my notes one last time
sentences.target_language: Mientras esperaba repasé mis apuntes por última vez
sentences.grammarNote:
**Mientras esperaba** = while I was waiting — **mientras** + imperfect for background/ongoing action.
**Repasé** = I went over/reviewed — preterite (completed action) of **repasar** (to review/go over).
The contrast here is classic: imperfect (**esperaba** — ongoing) + preterite (**repasé** — completed within that time).
**Los apuntes** = notes (taken in class/meeting) — plural, from **apuntar** (to jot down).
**Por última vez** = for the last time — a fixed phrase.

sentences.base_language: The interviewer came out and shook my hand firmly
sentences.target_language: El entrevistador salió y me dio un firme apretón de manos
sentences.grammarNote:
**El entrevistador / la entrevistadora** = interviewer — from **entrevistar** (to interview).
**Salió** = came out — preterite of **salir** (irregular): **salí, saliste, salió, salimos, salisteis, salieron**.
**Me dio** = gave me — preterite of **dar** (irregular): **di, diste, dio, dimos, disteis, dieron**.
**Un apretón de manos** = a handshake — literally "a squeeze of hands".

sentences.base_language: He asked me to tell him a little about myself
sentences.target_language: Me pidió que le contara un poco sobre mí mismo
sentences.grammarNote:
**Me pidió que + imperfect subjunctive** — asking/requesting in past reported speech triggers the subjunctive.
**Pidió** = asked/requested — preterite of **pedir** (e→i stem-changer): **pedí, pediste, pidió, pedimos, pedisteis, pidieron**.
**Le contara** = imperfect subjunctive of **contar** (to tell/recount) — **o→ue** in present, but the imperfect subjunctive uses the preterite stem.
**Mí mismo** = myself — **mí** (stressed) after a preposition: **sobre mí** (about me).

sentences.base_language: I explained that I had been working in marketing for five years
sentences.target_language: Expliqué que llevaba cinco años trabajando en marketing
sentences.grammarNote:
**Expliqué** = I explained — preterite of **explicar** (spelling change: **c → qu** before **e**: **expliqué**).
**Llevaba cinco años trabajando** = I had been working for five years — **llevaba + number + gerund** expresses duration of an ongoing past action.
This is the past version of: **llevo cinco años trabajando** (I've been working for five years — present).
**En marketing** — anglicism used directly in Spanish.

sentences.base_language: He wanted to know what my greatest weakness was
sentences.target_language: Quería saber cuál era mi mayor debilidad
sentences.grammarNote:
**Quería** = wanted — imperfect of **querer** (e→ie), used for ongoing desire/intent in the past.
**Saber** = to know (facts/information) — distinct from **conocer** (to know people/places).
**Cuál era** — indirect question in past: **cuál** (which/what) + imperfect.
**Mayor** = greatest/largest — the comparative/superlative of **grande**: **mayor debilidad** (greatest weakness).
**La debilidad** = weakness — from **débil** (weak).

sentences.base_language: I said that I tended to take on too many projects at once but that I was working on it
sentences.target_language: Dije que tendía a asumir demasiados proyectos a la vez pero que estaba trabajando en ello
sentences.grammarNote:
**Dije** = I said — preterite of **decir** (highly irregular): **dije, dijiste, dijo, dijimos, dijisteis, dijeron**.
**Tendía** = I tended — imperfect of **tender** (e→ie), here in its meaning of "to tend to".
**Tender a + infinitive** = to tend to do something.
**Demasiados** = too many — agrees with masculine plural: **demasiados proyectos**, **demasiadas reuniones**.
**Ello** = it (neuter pronoun) — refers back to the general situation: **trabajando en ello** (working on it).

sentences.base_language: He asked what I would do if a client rejected my proposal
sentences.target_language: Me preguntó qué haría yo si un cliente rechazara mi propuesta
sentences.grammarNote:
**Si + imperfect subjunctive... conditional** = if... would — this is the "second conditional" in Spanish, for hypothetical situations.
**Si rechazara** (if he/she rejected) — imperfect subjunctive.
**Haría** = would do — conditional of **hacer**: **haría, harías, haría, haríamos, haríais, harían**.
**Yo** is included for emphasis (subject pronouns are usually omitted in Spanish).
**Rechazara** — from **rechazar** (to reject, to turn down).

sentences.base_language: I told him I would try to understand their concerns before presenting a revised version
sentences.target_language: Le dije que intentaría entender sus preocupaciones antes de presentar una versión revisada
sentences.grammarNote:
**Le dije que + conditional** — in reported speech after a past verb, future becomes conditional.
**Intentaría** = I would try — conditional of **intentar** (regular -ar): add **-ía** to the infinitive.
**Antes de + infinitive** = before doing something: **antes de presentar** (before presenting).
**Revisada** = revised — past participle of **revisar** used as adjective; agrees with **versión** (feminine).

sentences.base_language: It seemed like that answer impressed him
sentences.target_language: Pareció que esa respuesta le había impresionado
sentences.grammarNote:
**Pareció** = it seemed — preterite of **parecer** (to seem/appear), third-person singular.
**Le había impresionado** = had impressed him — past perfect (pluperfect): **haber** (imperfect) + past participle.
The pluperfect indicates an action completed before another past action.
**Esa respuesta** = that answer — **esa** (that, demonstrative, feminine) agrees with **respuesta**.

sentences.base_language: Then he showed me a case study and asked me to analyze it
sentences.target_language: Luego me mostró un caso práctico y me pidió que lo analizara
sentences.grammarNote:
**Mostró** = showed — preterite of **mostrar** (o→ue): **mostré, mostraste, mostró...**
**Me pidió que + imperfect subjunctive** — requesting in the past: the subjunctive is required after verbs of asking/requesting.
**Lo analizara** = analyze it — **lo** refers back to **caso práctico** (masculine); **analizara** is imperfect subjunctive of **analizar**.
**Un caso práctico** = a case study — literally "a practical case".

sentences.base_language: I had studied something similar so I knew what approach to take
sentences.target_language: Había estudiado algo parecido así que supe qué enfoque tomar
sentences.grammarNote:
**Había estudiado** = I had studied — past perfect (pluperfect): **había** (imperfect of **haber**) + past participle.
Used for actions completed before another past event.
**Supe** = I found out / I knew (in that moment) — preterite of **saber**: knowing a fact (instantaneous realization).
Contrast: **sabía** (imperfect) = I knew (ongoing background knowledge) vs. **supe** = I came to know (realization).
**El enfoque** = the approach/focus.

sentences.base_language: At the end he asked if I had any questions for him
sentences.target_language: Al final me preguntó si yo tenía alguna pregunta para él
sentences.grammarNote:
**Al final** = at the end — **al** = a + el. Compare: **al principio** (at the beginning), **al final** (at the end).
**Si + imperfect** in indirect question — reporting a yes/no question in the past uses **si** + imperfect: **preguntó si tenía** (asked if I had).
**Alguna pregunta** = any question — **alguna** (feminine singular) agrees with **pregunta**.

sentences.base_language: I asked about the team I would be working with
sentences.target_language: Pregunté sobre el equipo con el que trabajaría
sentences.grammarNote:
**Pregunté** = I asked — preterite of **preguntar** (regular -ar).
**Con el que** = with which/that I — a relative pronoun phrase. **El que** agrees with **equipo** (masculine singular): **con el que** (with which).
Could also say **con quien** if referring to people: **el equipo con quien trabajaría**.
**Trabajaría** = I would work — conditional, expressing future-in-the-past.

sentences.base_language: And I asked what he valued most in a candidate
sentences.target_language: Y pregunté qué era lo que más valoraba en un candidato
sentences.grammarNote:
**Lo que más valoraba** = what he valued most — **lo que** = what/that which (neuter relative).
**Lo** is the neuter article used before **que** when no specific noun is referenced.
**Valoraba** = valued — imperfect of **valorar** (regular -ar), for habitual/ongoing past action.
**El candidato / la candidata** = candidate — gender forms differ.

sentences.base_language: He said he was looking for someone who wasn't afraid of making mistakes
sentences.target_language: Dijo que buscaba a alguien que no tuviera miedo a equivocarse
sentences.grammarNote:
**Dijo que + imperfect** — reported speech: past verb → imperfect in the subordinate clause.
**Buscaba a alguien que** — the personal **a** before **alguien** (someone); **que** introduces a relative clause.
**No tuviera miedo** = wasn't afraid — subjunctive because **alguien** is indefinite/hypothetical.
**Tener miedo a / de** = to be afraid of — **miedo a equivocarse** (afraid of making mistakes).
**Equivocarse** = to make a mistake / to be wrong — reflexive verb.

sentences.base_language: He told me they would let me know within a week
sentences.target_language: Me dijo que me avisarían en el plazo de una semana
sentences.grammarNote:
**Me dijo que + conditional** — future in reported past speech becomes conditional.
**Avisarían** = they would let me know/notify — conditional plural of **avisar** (to notify/warn).
**En el plazo de** = within (a time period) — a formal expression: **en el plazo de una semana** (within a week), **en el plazo de tres días** (within three days).

sentences.base_language: I left feeling it had gone well but without being overconfident
sentences.target_language: Salí sintiendo que había ido bien pero sin estar demasiado confiado
sentences.grammarNote:
**Salí** = I left — preterite of **salir** (irregular: **salí, saliste, salió...**).
**Sintiendo** = feeling — gerund of **sentir** (e→i stem-changer); gerunds are used after certain verbs to describe simultaneous action.
**Había ido** = had gone — pluperfect: **había** + past participle **ido** (from **ir**).
**Sin estar** = without being — **sin + infinitive** (without doing): **sin pensar** (without thinking), **sin saber** (without knowing).
**Demasiado** = too (much) — adverb before adjective: **demasiado confiado** (too confident).

sentences.base_language: Three days later they called and offered me the position
sentences.target_language: Tres días después me llamaron y me ofrecieron el puesto
sentences.grammarNote:
**Tres días después** = three days later — **después** (after/later) follows the time expression.
Contrast: **antes** (before/earlier): **dos días antes** (two days before).
**Me llamaron** = they called me — **llamar** (regular -ar), preterite third-person plural.
**Me ofrecieron** = they offered me — preterite of **ofrecer** (irregular yo: **ofrezco**): **ofrecí, ofreciste, ofreció, ofrecimos, ofrecieron**.
**El puesto** = the position/post.
